tweets.text:
unpopular opinion: you should make every major life decision in under 72 hours.

the person agonising for 6 months over whether to quit, move, start, or leave already knows the answer. they knew in the first 10 minutes. the remaining 5 months and 29 days is just fear dressed up as due diligence.

"but what if i make the wrong choice?"

wrong choices are correctable. indecision is not. you can recover from a bad decision in weeks. you cannot recover the years you spent avoiding one.

the cost of a wrong decision is almost always lower than the cost of no decision. always.

more is lost by indecision than wrong decision.

the people who move fast dont have more certainty than you. they just have less tolerance for standing still.

yes! And even more lax than that. These rules are mean to work even in HOSTILE territory 

Good faith actors will choose to be candid, yes, and we set the expectation for what is fair to disclose. But I am allowed to pry and "read your mind" (invade your privacy) if I know you
tweets.name: Universal Public Defender

tweets.text:
proposed rule - it's okay to screenshot and share a DM, as long as you are sharing YOUR WORDS ONLY and not the other person 

(in other words, I don't need your permission to tell my trust network that I am talking to you & what I said to you. That is my information)

tweets.text:
yes I think this is appropriate. I am designing these rules with the idea that it will remain unregulated for the forseeable future (I am counting on "good faith actors" adopting it, while bad faith actors are aware of it but choose not to adopt it)

we can ask the more powerful party to disclose, but cannot compel them (fundamentally because: we may not even know they exist!) - but we should be allowed to regulate our own networks, do this internal self surveillance and origin tracing, and IF it reveals something confidential, I shouldn't be punished for that. Because I only did that in defense / in my attempt to secure the boundaries of my information network
tweets.name: Universal Public Defender
